Preconditions: User is logged in; Destination search page is displayed
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Enter a minimum price of $10.00 and maximum price of $50.00 in the price range fields (optional)
2. Step 2: Select a destination from the dropdown list (e.g., New York) and check that it's not mandatory
3. Step 3: Click the 'Search' button to submit the search query
4. Step 4: Verify that the system returns a list of restaurants within the specified price range and destination, with optional fields populated
5. Step 5: Check that the price range fields are still populated with the original values (if entered)
6. Step 6: Attempt to enter an invalid destination and verify that an error message is displayed
Expected Result: The system accepts optional fields for destination search and displays relevant results.
Test Data: {"actual_min_price": "$10.00", "actual_max_price": "$50.00", "expected_result": "The system returns a list of restaurants within the specified price range and destination, with optional fields populated, and no more than 100 results, within 2 seconds"}

Test Title: Search by Restaurant Type: SQL Injection
Description: Validate that the search functionality does not allow SQL injection attacks when searching by restaurant type.
Preconditions: The mobile app is installed and launched
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Launch the mobile app and navigate to the search page
2. Step 2: Enter a malicious input in the 'Restaurant Type' field, such as ' OR 1=1 --'
3. Step 3: Verify that the search results are returned without any errors or exceptions
4. Step 4: Inspect the network traffic using a tool like Burp Suite to verify that the injected SQL is not executed
5. Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 with different malicious inputs, such as ' OR 1=2 --' and ' OR 1=3 --'
6. Step 6: Verify that the search results are still returned without any errors or exceptions
Expected Result: The search results are returned without any errors or exceptions, and the injected SQL is not executed.

Test Title: Search by Restaurant Type: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)

Preconditions: The mobile app is installed and launched
Test Steps:

2. Step 2: Enter a malicious input in the 'Restaurant Type' field, such as '<script>alert('XSS')</script>'
3. Step 3: Verify that the search results are returned without any errors or exceptions
4. Step 4: Inspect the HTML source code of the search results page to verify that the injected script is not executed
5. Step 5: Repeat steps 2-4 with different malicious inputs, such as '<script>alert('XSS')</script>' and '<script>alert('XSS')</script>'
6. Step 6: Verify that the search results are still returned without any errors or exceptions
Expected Result: The search results are returned without any errors or exceptions, and the injected script is not executed.

Test Title: Search by Restaurant Type: Authentication Bypass
Description: Validate that the search functionality does not allow authentication bypass when searching by restaurant type.

Test Title: New Release Notification Performance Test
Description: Verifies that the system can handle a large number of users downloading new releases within a specified time frame.
Preconditions: System is configured to send notifications for new releases; Users are logged in and have access to the release notification feature
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Simulate 1000 users logging in and accessing their account dashboard
2. Step 2: Trigger a new release notification with 10 new releases available
3. Step 3: Measure the average time it takes for each user to receive the notification (max 5 seconds)
4. Step 4: Verify that all users receive the notification within the specified time frame (95% of users should receive the notification within 2 seconds, 100% within 5 seconds)
5. Step 5: Measure the average time it takes for each user to download a new release (max 10 seconds)
6. Step 6: Verify that all users can successfully download at least one new release within the specified time frame (95% of users should complete the download within 2 seconds, 100% within 5 seconds)
Expected Result: All users receive the notification and can successfully download at least one new release within 5 seconds. Average time to receive notification: 2.5 seconds, average time to download a new release: 4.8 seconds.

Test Title: New Release Download Performance Test
Description: Verifies that the system can handle a large number of users downloading new releases simultaneously.
Preconditions: System is configured to send notifications for new releases; Users are logged in and have access to the release notification feature
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Simulate 500 users attempting to download a new release simultaneously
2. Step 2: Measure the average time it takes for each user to start downloading (max 3 seconds)
3. Step 3: Verify that all users can successfully complete their downloads within the specified time frame (95% of users should complete the download within 5 seconds, 100% within 10 seconds)
4. Step 4: Measure the average time it takes for each user to finish downloading (max 15 seconds)
5. Step 5: Verify that the system can handle the simultaneous downloads without any errors or performance degradation
6. Step 6: Repeat steps 1-5 with increasing numbers of users to verify scalability
Expected Result: All users can successfully complete their downloads within 10 seconds. Average time to start downloading: 1.8 seconds, average time to finish downloading: 6.2 seconds.

Test Title: New Release Notification and Download Performance Test
Description: Verifies that the system can handle a large number of users receiving notifications and downloading new releases simultaneously.
Preconditions: System is configured to send notifications for new releases; Users are logged in and have access to the release notification feature
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Simulate 2000 users logging in and accessing their account dashboard
2. Step 2: Trigger a new release notification with 20 new releases available
3. Step 3: Measure the average time it takes for each user to receive the notification (max 5 seconds)
4. Step 4: Verify that all users can successfully download at least one new release within the specified time frame (95% of users should complete the download within 10 seconds, 100% within 15 seconds)
5. Step 5: Measure the average time it takes for each user to finish downloading (max 20 seconds)
6. Step 6: Verify that the system can handle the simultaneous notifications and downloads without any errors or performance degradation
Expected Result: All users receive the notification and can successfully download at least one new release within 15 seconds. Average time to receive notification: 2.5 seconds, average time to finish downloading: 8.1 seconds.

Test Title: Unauthorized access to new release notifications
Description: Validate that only authorized users can receive new release notifications
Preconditions: User is not logged in
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Send a request to the notification endpoint without authentication
2. Step 2: Verify that the response status code is 401 Unauthorized
3. Step 3: Verify that the response body contains an error message indicating unauthorized access
4. Step 4: Log in as an authorized user and send another request to the notification endpoint
5. Step 5: Verify that the response status code is 200 OK and the new release notification is present
Expected Result: The unauthorized user receives a 401 Unauthorized response, while the authorized user receives a 200 OK response with new release notification.

Test Title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in new release notifications
Description: Validate that the notification endpoint is not vulnerable to XSS attacks
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Craft a malicious payload with JavaScript code and send it as part of the request body
2. Step 2: Verify that the response status code is 200 OK and the new release notification is present
3. Step 3: Inspect the response HTML for any signs of script injection or execution
4. Step 4: Verify that no JavaScript code is executed in the browser when receiving the notification
5. Step 5: Repeat steps 1-4 with different malicious payloads to ensure robustness
Expected Result: The response HTML does not contain any signs of script injection or execution, and no JavaScript code is executed in the browser.

Test Title: Data leakage: unauthorized access to new release metadata
Description: Validate that sensitive information about new releases is not exposed to unauthorized users
Preconditions: User is not logged in
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Send a request to the notification endpoint with an invalid JWT token
2. Step 2: Verify that the response status code is 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden
3. Step 3: Verify that no sensitive information about new releases is present in the response body
4. Step 4: Log in as an authorized user and send another request to the notification endpoint
5. Step 5: Verify that only authorized users can access the metadata of new releases
Expected Result: Unauthorized users receive a 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden response, while authorized users can access the metadata of new releases.

Test Title: New Release Notification - User Download
Description: Verify that users receive notifications when a new release is available for download.
Preconditions: User has an active account; Restaurant/Food Service API is accessible
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Send GET request to /api/releases with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
3. Step 3: Extract release ID from response body and store it as 'release_id'
4. Step 4: Send GET request to /api/releases/{release_id}/download with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
5. Step 5: Verify HTTP 302 status code returned and redirect URL points to the download page
6. Step 6: Verify that the user receives a notification about the new release availability
Expected Result: The user receives a notification with the exact text 'New release available for download!' and is redirected to the download page.

Test Title: Profile Page: Mandatory vs Optional Field Validation
Description: Validate mandatory and optional fields on the profile page.

4. Step 4: Attempt to update a profile with missing optional field (e.g., phone number).
5. Step 5: Verify the updated profile still reflects the original data, as the optional field was not required.
Expected Result: The mandatory and optional fields on the profile page are validated correctly. Mandatory fields must be filled in, while optional fields can be left blank without affecting the profile creation or update.

Test Title: Profile Page: Data Type Constraints (Integer, String)

5. Step 5: Verify the updated profile still reflects the original data, as the invalid input was not accepted.
Expected Result: The data type constraints for integer and string fields on the profile page are validated correctly. Integer fields require numerical values, while string fields accept only valid text inputs.

Test Title: Account Creation Performance Test: Valid User Input
Description: This test validates the performance of creating a new account with valid user input.
Preconditions: The system is up and running; No existing accounts
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Send POST request to /create-account endpoint with valid username, email, password, and confirm_password
2. Step 2: Verify the response time does not exceed 500ms
3. Step 3: Validate the account creation process completes within 5 seconds
4. Step 4: Verify the new account is successfully created and appears in the user list
5. Step 5: Repeat steps 1-4 with varying numbers of concurrent users (up to 10) to ensure performance remains stable
Expected Result: The account creation process completes within 5 seconds, and the new account is successfully created with a response time of less than or equal to 500ms.

Test Title: Account Creation Performance Test: Invalid User Input
Description: This test validates the performance of creating a new account with invalid user input.
Preconditions: The system is up and running; No existing accounts
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Send POST request to /create-account endpoint with invalid username (less than 3 characters), email, password, and confirm_password
2. Step 2: Verify the response time does not exceed 500ms
3. Step 3: Validate the account creation process completes within 5 seconds
4. Step 4: Verify an error message is displayed indicating invalid input
5. Step 5: Repeat steps 1-4 with varying numbers of concurrent users (up to 10) to ensure performance remains stable
Expected Result: The account creation process completes within 5 seconds, and an error message is displayed indicating invalid input with a response time of less than or equal to 500ms.

Test Title: Account Creation Performance Test: Concurrent Users
Description: This test validates the performance of creating multiple accounts concurrently.
Preconditions: The system is up and running; No existing accounts
Test Steps:
1. Step 1: Send POST requests to /create-account endpoint with valid username, email, password, and confirm_password for 5 concurrent users
2. Step 2: Verify the response time does not exceed 1000ms
3. Step 3: Validate the account creation process completes within 10 seconds
4. Step 4: Verify all 5 accounts are successfully created and appear in the user list
5. Step 5: Repeat steps 1-4 with varying numbers of concurrent users (up to 20) to ensure performance remains stable
Expected Result: The account creation process completes within 10 seconds for up to 20 concurrent users, and all accounts are successfully created with a response time of less than or equal to 1000ms.
